notes: Run 5 — full 13-transcript corpus expansion (5 academic + 8 tech-leader-talk transcripts spanning 7 distinct tech leaders) with relaxed atom quality filter and cross-LEADER (not just cross-transcript) brainstorm constraint. Standing instruction from user: phases continuous. Run 5 acknowledges that prior intermediate runs (run_002..run_004) were attempted in earlier sessions but were not persisted to the repository; the only previously-committed run is run_001. The user-labeled count cited in the prompt (14 trivial labels) is taken on report; on-disk impact_labels.json only contains the 3 labels committed in commit 6c3d54df. The Run 5 design uses run_001 as its on-disk comparison baseline and treats the user-reported 14-label trivia signal as the substantive prior.
expansion_provenance: Tech-leader transcripts (T007..T014) committed in ece58074: '7 tech leaders transcript fetched locally'. Sandbox auto-fetch dead; user fetched locally and pushed.

phase_modifications_from_run_001: PHASE 1: paradigm_shift/atom_quality_filter.py — keep atom if >=1 positive feature OR is open_problem/first_principle type. Positive vocabulary includes AGI, scaling laws, world model, JEPA, software 2.0/3.0, agent, distillation, distributed, multi-modal, embodied, simulator.

PHASE 2: paradigm_shift/speaker_self_publish_cache.json — pre-built map of tech-leader published papers/posts (Karpathy Software 2.0/3.0/intro-to-LLMs, Sutton Bitter Lesson, Hinton Forward-Forward arXiv:2212.13345, LeCun A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence + JEPA arXiv:2301.08243, Naval no papers). Used by market_verifier v2 to detect when a candidate's atoms are restating the speaker's own published prior art.

PHASE 3: analogy_engine.brainstorm_paradigm_candidates — require candidate.atoms come from >=2 DIFFERENT speaker_ids (not just different transcript_ids). Karpathy x Karpathy (T007 x T013) and Hinton x Hinton (T010 x T012) are now rejected even though cross-transcript. Bias toward (academic, tech_leader) and (tech_leader, tech_leader) atom pairs; reject same-speaker pairs.
mechanism_validation_targets: MV-1..MV-5 from run_001 still apply (6 layers complete).
CL-1: Layer 4 (brainstorm) produces >0 candidates under cross-leader constraint.
CL-2: At least 1 candidate uses an (academic, tech_leader) atom pair.
CL-3: At least 1 candidate uses a (tech_leader, tech_leader) atom pair where speakers are distinct.
QF-1: Atom quality filter retains >0 atoms per tech-leader transcript.
SP-1: speaker_self_publish_cache flags >=1 atom that paraphrases the speaker's own paper.
honest_expectation: Run 5 is the first run on a corpus that matches paradigm_shift_finder's intended distribution (tech-leader vision content). The 14 trivial labels reported across Runs 2-4 suggest the cross-transcript-only constraint was insufficient: cross-leader is the next experimental knob. We do NOT expect S-1 to be met automatically; we expect Run 5 to surface (a) at least one cross-leader candidate with non-trivial structure, and (b) the speaker_self_publish_cache to demonstrate that Karpathy x Karpathy or Hinton x Hinton style intra-leader pairs would have been a major failure mode if not excluded. Without a real WebSearch hook, Layer 5 will reject everything via FAIL_RAG_UNGROUNDED — that is identical to Run 1 and is acknowledged.
